appraisal_events.evidence: « pour vous si intelligent, cela m’étonne que vous ne compreniez pas qu’il ne faut pas discuter ce qui fait plaisir à ses amis mais le faire… je sais que vous ne l’auriez pas fait. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator directly rebukes Robert for discussing rather than promptly doing a favor and suggests he would fail a stronger test (arranging a dinner with the duchess). The passage frames this reproach through the narrator’s own admitted manipulative motives, which ironizes the blame.

status_effects.explanation: Locally, Robert is cast as falling short of ideal friendship and practical effectiveness, lowering his standing in the narrator’s evaluative speech.
